name: Pilfer (TIER 0 Stealth)
chain: Pilfer → Stealth → Misdirection
description: You are now able to redirect attention, perform sleight-of-hand tricks, pickpocket, and plant. ● This is a non-combat only skill. ● Please consult the GM about any situational modifiers affecting you.

name: Stealth (TIER 1 Stealth)
chain: Pilfer → Stealth → Misdirection
description: You are now skilled enough to hide, sneak, and set ambushes. ● This is a non-combat only skill. ● Stealth is broken if you are detected or if you attack or defend if combat has begun. ● Please consult the GM about any situational modifiers affecting you.

name: Misdirection (TIER 2 Stealth)
chain: Pilfer → Stealth → Misdirection
description: You are now skilled enough to perform stealth actions while in combat. You are able to hide, sneak, ambush, and steal. ● Dropping into stealth during combat costs 1 AP if successful. ● Every aggressive action during combat thereafter costs an additional 1 AP. ○ This includes attacks, backstabs, planting, initiating a trap, etc. ● You CANNOT use this skill to steal things directly out of an enemy’s hands. ● You CANNOT use this skill while you are directly engaged (needing to clash) or restrained. ● You CANNOT use this skill when in a fight by yourself unless using a magical assist. ● Please consult the GM about any situational modifiers affecting you. ● Attacking or defending in combat will BREAK STEALTH for the rest of the fight. ○ This is ONLY for the hidden OC, and does not include stealthed OCs whose companions or animals are acting. ○ Some spells are capable of augmenting stealth or can be used without breaking stealth. This is at GM discretion. These spells do NOT gain ambush bonuses unless narratively sensible. ● AMBUSH BONUSES: ○ When ambushing an enemy from stealth who cannot detect you, they will ALWAYS only be capable of partial reactions even if their Reaction Score would normally beat your attack speed. ○ Instead of clashing at 70% power, if they can still perform a partial reaction, they will clash at 50% power. Character Skills

name: Riding & Harness Driving
chain: None
description: You are able to use mounts, and drive carts and carriages. You are also able to use any tool or vehicle requiring a yoke or harness. Rough terrain or other challenges are likely to require classical mounts. ● You are able to ride as a passenger without this skill. This skill is required to drive.

name: Pilot
chain: None
description: You are able to pilot basic vehicles, including unpowered boats, motorbikes, and simple vehicles. ● You are able to ride as a passenger without this skill. This skill is required to drive. ● Complex steam-based vehicles REQUIRE Steam Engines skill. ● Aerial vehicles REQUIRE Gasworks skill.

name: Magic Tool Use
chain: None
description: You are able to focus mana to activate enchanted tools. ● Mages with Mana Circuits get this skill for free. ● Anyone with Magic Background gets this skill for free.

name: Pacing
chain: None
description: You are able to focus more on the job at hand. ● Permanent +1 AP in all scenes.

name: Quartermastery
chain: None
description: With experience, you’ve learned how to better manage your pack. ● Permanent +2 CC in all scenes.

name: Adept
prereq_raw: At least 4 Combat OR Class Skills
chain: Adept → Operative → Veteran
description: Unlocking Adept is a show of skill, meaning you are no longer a green recruit. ● This skill unlocks greater action economy. ○ +1 Action ○ +1 Reaction

name: Operative
prereq_raw: At least 6 Combat OR Class, +2 Knowledge
chain: Adept → Operative → Veteran
description: Unlocking Operative requires you to be a fully fledged, dependable mercenary. ● This skill unlocks greater action economy. ○ +1 Action ○ +1 Reaction ○ +1 Bonus Action ○ +1 Free Action

name: Veteran
prereq_raw: At least 8 Combat OR Class, +4 Knowledge
chain: Adept → Operative → Veteran
description: Unlocking Specialist means you are a natural-born leader and trusted operative within the company. ● This skill unlocks greater action economy. ○ +1 Action ○ +1 Reaction

name: Linguistics
description: You have studied language syntax and its use, both formal and casual, written and spoken. ● Able to translate and read “Old Republic” writings, frequently found in historical documents and ruins. ● Able to translate Republic “legalese.” ● +2 to Language, Translation, and Dialect Skill Checks

name: Mathematics
description: You are a studious mathematician, and you excel at applying your theories in the everyday. ● Your character MUST have an educated or gifted background, or significant RP to support this trait. ● Because you can easily calculate arrow/bullet drop and projectile motion: ○ Gain a permanent +5% max range with firearms. ○ Gain a permanent +10% max range with simple ranged weapons. ● +2 to all mathematical, spatial, and encryption skill checks.

name: Physics
description: You are thrilled by the mechanical laws of nature, and you excel at applying your theories in the everyday. ● Your character MUST have an educated or gifted background, or significant RP to support this trait. ● Because you naturally understand applied force: ○ Gain a permanent +2% output bonus with melee weapons. ● +2 to all physics and kinetic design skill checks

name: Chemistry
description: You are thrilled by the most logical ‘magic’ there is, and you excel at applying your theories in the everyday. ● Your character MUST have an educated or gifted background, or significant RP to support this trait. ● You are better at making reliable medications and chemical concoctions. ○ When creating recipes for medicines or chemistry-based concoctions, you may request an additional minor trait or bonus for your creations. ● +2 to all chemistry, alchemical, and reactive herbalism skills.

name: Geology
description: It’s not dirt to you – it’s history. A rock is never a rock – it’s a crucial resource with boundless applications. ● Your character MUST have an educated, gifted, or relevant background in spelunking, prospecting, surveying, or mining; or, significant RP to support this trait. ● You know where every type of mineral or soil is found in Doranswyr, and can identify them all. ● You can ascertain the authenticity and value of jewels and rare minerals. ● You may ask the GM for ONE hint relying on geology when in an underground or related scene. ● +2 to all geology, mineral, stone, gem, and soil skill checks.

name: Biology
description: The study of life isn’t just a field for mages. You understand biological functions, anatomy, and biochemistry, and understand there is a microcosmos that much of the public doesn’t believe in. ● Your character MUST have an educated or gifted background, or significant RP to support this trait. ● You are more gifted at diagnosis, and can recognize common diseases and disorders just by looking at a victim or a sample. ● +2 to all general biology checks. Religious & Cultural Skills

name: Catechumen
description: You have a layman’s understanding of a given faith. ● You can obtain this skill through significant roleplay. ● This skill is free at character creation to DEVOUT characters only. ● Grants LORE ACCESS to the chosen faith. ● +2 to faith-based knowledge checks.

name: Luminary
description: You have obtained a scholarly or ordained level of knowledge with a given faith, and understand their rituals, secrets, and sacred texts. ● You can obtain this skill through significant roleplay. ● This skill is available (not free) at character creation to ORDAINED characters only. ● This skill is REQUIRED to formally RP as an ordained member of a faith. ● Grants higher-level LORE ACCESS to the chosen faith. ● +4 to faith-based knowledge checks.

name: Doranswyr Historian
description: A keen interest in the past has led to substantial study of national history. You know more than the average bear about important figures, global politics, and the history of the Doranswyr Republic. ● Grants higher-level LORE ACCESS to Doranswyr. ● +2 to historical skill checks.

name: Local Study: City-States
description: You understand the local customs, culture, and history of a particular region; this includes who the important players are, how trade is conducted, and even knowledge about crime. ● Grants LORE ACCESS to the selected city-state. ● This skill is FREE with an Origin trait. ● You can take this skill multiple times for different city-states, with substantial roleplay. ● +4 to any skill check relating to the city-state, including “streetwise” type checks

name: Martial Historian
description: You took a keen interest in martial history, studying not only battle outcomes, but the tactics used and why they succeeded and failed. ● You may ask for one field strategy boon from the GM when entering open-field battles. ● +4 to all combat, martial, and strategy skill checks Skilled Trade Skills

name: Print Forgery
description: You have skillful handwriting, have practiced calligraphy, and can genuinely copy others’ patterns. ● You are better at spotting forgeries and ascertaining the authenticity of genuine articles. ● You are able to make forgeries – the quality of which is determined by roll. ● +4 to forgery-related skill checks

name: Cartography
description: You are skilled at remembering locations & terrain and have the ability to readily recreate it in the form of maps. ● You are able to create maps of places you been to (Missions and Events) ● With the maps you can recall locations and figure out if there are secret passage ways and can tell if a place was there always or not ● +4 to map/location-related skill checks

name: Codebreaking
description: You have studied syntax and patterns so much that you have become skills with making and breaking language ciphers. You can also decode unknown languages more easily. ● You are able to request a cipher hint from GMs once per scene for free. ● Subsequent requests must be rolled for. ● +4 to all cipher, encryption, and translation checks

name: Automata Design
description: Gears, servos, stepper motors… if it moves, you’ve taken it apart and put it back together again. ● REQUIRES Mathematics OR Physics, OR significant RP ● You have a unique understanding of automata and know their weaknesses and strengths. ● You gain +5% output against automata. Includes Forgeborn exoskeletons. ● +4 to all skill checks dealing with automata.

name: Steam Engines
description: You know how to sooth temperamental steam engines, boilers, hydraulics, and pressure-based engines. ● REQUIRES Chemistry OR Physics, OR significant RP ● You are able to repair or sabotage steam-based technology with injury. ● You are able to pilot steam-based engines without risk. ● As Forgeborn: You are able to make safe steam-powered inventions. ● +4 to all skill checks dealing with steam technology.

name: Gasworks
description: Before electricity, the cities were lit by thousands of miles of gas lines – and most still are. ● REQUIRES Chemistry, OR significant RP ● You can repair or sabotage lines safely. ● You can detect and identify gasses naturally. ● You know how to find gas lines and work with gas. ● As Forgeborn: You are able to make safe gas-powered inventions. ● +4 to all skill checks dealing with gas technology.

name: Electricity
description: The newest and most dangerous invention, you are fascinated by this power that is indistinguishable from magic. ● REQUIRES Physics, OR significant RP ● You can safely overload and reroute power, work with electricity, or and work on electric devices. ● As Forgeborn: You are able to make safe electric inventions. ● +4 to all skill checks dealing with electricity.

name: Metallurgy
description: Metals, alloys, and all of their properties – you understand them implicitly, and have a wealth of skill at finding new applications for them. ● REQUIRES Geology, OR significant RP ● Your forged weapons and items have better durability and keener edges than anyone else’s. ○ Choose between either: ■ +2% Output Bonus ■ +10% Item Durability ● +4 to all skill checks dealing with metals and alloys.

name: Veterinary Study
description: You’ve studied animal physiology and behavior, and understand how their bodies work. ● REQUIRES Biology, OR significant RP ● You are able to use Field Medic skills on animals with no penalty. ● +4 to all skill checks dealing with animal physiology, behavior, diets, and anatomy.

name: Basic Medkits
chain: Basic Medkits→ Advanced Medkits
description: You are able to craft and use basic medkits. You understand what should be in them and how every tool should be used. The most common basic medkits are first aid kits that can treat shallow wounds and illnesses. Medkits are labeled as Basic or Advanced, and may be used to treat different types of ailments. When using Medkits, you are not able to attack or react.

name: Advanced Medkits
chain: Basic Medkits→ Advanced Medkits
description: You are able to craft and use advanced medkits. You understand what should be in them and how every tool should be used. The most common advanced medkits are surgical kits that can be used to treat grievous wounds in the field. Medkits are labeled as Basic or Advanced, and may be used to treat different types of ailments. When using Medkits, you are not able to attack or react.

name: Prepared Medic I
chain: Prepared Medic I → Prepared Medic II → Prepared Medic III
description: You have been mentored on how to best pack your medical supplies for quick access in the field. ● You can carry twice the number of Basic medkits. ( WU x 0.5 on all basic medkits)

name: Prepared Medic II
chain: Prepared Medic I → Prepared Medic II → Prepared Medic III
description: You’re learning how to tightly pack medical supplies without compromising them. ● You can now carry twice the number of Advanced medkits. ( WU x 0.5 on all advanced medkits)

name: Prepared Medic III
chain: Prepared Medic I → Prepared Medic II → Prepared Medic III
description: You are no stranger to your kits – you can unpack them and reorganize them without losing track of where anything is kept on your person, and it’s almost as if they’re not even there. ● You can carry 1 Basic and 1 Advanced medkit FOR FREE. ● Additional medkits of each type still weigh half as much as normal. Medical Training Skills

name: Stabilization
chain: None
description: Stabilization is the practice of advanced emergency triage. Stabilization is faster than trying to completely treat a wound with a medkit – instead, the focus is simply on salvaging living tissue as quickly as possible, and is ideal in dangerous situations where there isn’t enough cover to allow a field medic to work. Stabilization will fully stop bleeding and arrest death, but does not treat wounds and cannot be used to revive patients. Medics with this skill can always stabilize, even if they do not have a medkit available. Every actively bleeding or life-threatening wound requires a separate medkit. Stabilization With Basic Medkits: ● Consumes the Medkit. ● Takes 2 Turns for T4 and below. ○ Stabilization is not possible for T5. ● Medic cannot attack or defend during this period. Stabilization With Advanced Medkits: ● Consumes the Medkit. ● Takes 1 Turn for T4 and below. ○ Takes 3 Turns for T5+. Simply beginning this process arrests death but it CANNOT be stopped once begun, or the patient will die. ● Medic cannot attack or defend during this period. Stabilization Without Medkits: ● Takes 1 Turn for T3 and below. ○ Takes 2 Turns for T4. ○ Stabilization is not possible for T5. ● Medic cannot attack or defend during this period.

name: Surgery
chain: None
description: You are more than just a medic – you’re a doctor. Or a bonesaw. Potato, potahto. Using an Advanced Medkit, you are able to perform surgical procedures outside of combat. Requires significant RP. These procedures include, but are not limited to: ● Cosmetic surgeries ● Reattachment of limbs ● Organ removal and transplants (okay, war criminal) Surgery success is based on a 1d20 roll. ● Nat 1: Critical Fail - Roll a 1d4 to see what Tier of injury you just dealt to your patient. ● 2 - 10: Surgery failure. Medkit is consumed, no change. ● 11+: Surgery success. ● Nat 20: Surgery success + you salvage your Advanced Medkit and do not consume it. Knowledge Skills and Luck modifiers support Surgical attempts.

name: Compound Medicine
chain: None
description: You have learned to create compound medicinals that are far more potent than anything a herbalist can create. You can combine medicines, perform assays, and study the properties of various medications. This is a crafting skill. To use this skill, you will need to create a recipe and a new medication, which must be submitted with your Recipe Journal. Staff will work with you on how to obtain the items you create through this skill, and how often you can create these items.

name: Tactical Orders I
chain: Tactical Orders I → Tactical Orders II → Tactical Orders III
description: You can issue clear, concise commands in the middle of combat that allies can immediately act on. These orders can be anyone of the 2 for one ally: ● You can target on ally boosting their clashing output by 7% ● Boost their Dodge by 10% At this level, your influence is limited to 1 person.

name: Tactical Orders II
chain: Tactical Orders I → Tactical Orders II → Tactical Orders III
description: Your authority and clarity improve, allowing you to direct 1-2 allies now. Not only has your orders you can display increased in target but also in power: ● You can target on ally boosting their clashing output by 14% ● Boost their Dodge by 15% Your presence begins to noticeably increase your team’s overall efficiency.

name: Tactical Orders III
chain: Tactical Orders I → Tactical Orders II → Tactical Orders III
description: You are no longer just giving orders but you are directing the flow of battle itself. You can now at max effect with 1-3 people with the increased power of your orders along with a new one. Must choose one for action: ● You can target on ally boosting their clashing output by 20% ● Boost their Dodge by 15% & Reaction ● Make an enemy the single target: If an ally attacks the target they get a 5% output bonus. At this level, your commands can shift the outcome of encounters when used correctly. Tracking and Strategy Skills

name: Target Tracking
chain: None
description: Through observation of terrain, disturbances, and behavioral patterns, you are able to track targets with a level of precision that borders on prediction. Footprints, broken foliage, displaced dust, and even the absence of movement all become information to you. You get a +1 to the following: ● Track individuals or groups across varied environments (urban, wilderness, battlefield aftermath) ● Determine approximate numbers, pace, and condition of those you are tracking ● Identify changes in movement such as splitting, regrouping, or attempts to hide their trail ● Predict likely routes, destinations, or ambush points based on terrain and behavior When assisting allies, gain a +3 to the following: ● Reducing the chance of being misled or ambushed while pursuing a target ● Improve the setup of ambushes by anticipating enemy movement ● Provide advance warnings of nearby threats before direct contact Knowledge Skills and Luck modifiers support Target Tracking.

name: Battlefield Awareness
chain: None
description: Movement, spacing, hesitation, aggression… It all tells a story. With enough focus, you can read that story in real time and act before danger fully unfolds. This skill allows you to attempt a Battlefield Read, predicting enemy actions and identifying threats before they resolve. Using Battlefield Awareness requires active focus and a callout to the GM. This can be done at the start of a turn or in response to a developing situation. Battlefield Read (1d20 Roll): ● Nat 1: Critical Fail - Roll a 1d4 [chosen by the gm], that your character will think is true ● 2 - 10: Failed Read - Nothing came to mind, no change. ● 11+: Successful Read - You can roll a 1d4 on what information you can learn to assist in combat ● Nat 20: Critical Success - You get to know all the information that can assist in a win in combat and can be based on a 1d3 roll to see if reinforcements are coming. Knowledge Skills and Luck modifiers support Battlefield Awareness.

name: Light Load
chain: Light Load → Hidden Compartments
description: With this skill smugglers can mark one item that is medium or smaller as lighter load. With it being counted as such they can then have its weighted value be set at zero. As smugglers always know how to move items into places they have no business Example: ● Shortsword/Rapier is normally weight at 2 and it a medium but with this skill it’s weighted at zero

name: Hidden Compartments
chain: Light Load → Hidden Compartments
description: Always a secret pocket, zipper or bag placed somewhere to hold items that weren’t thought to be there. Based on whichever bag/satchel they have on them. It will have a +2 of its weight value added. So the smuggler can carry more items for people or themselves. Example: ● Small Bag: 1+2=3 ● Medium Bag: 2+2=4 ● Large Bag: 3+2=5

name: Concealment
chain: Concealment → Mass Concealment
description: You are highly skilled in masking your presence, movements, and intent, allowing you to slip through spaces without drawing attention. Whether it’s through careful footwork, use of shadows, or reading the environment, you know how to make yourself difficult to notice even in active areas. This skill reflects practiced stealth rather than luck. You understand when to move, when to stay still, and how to control your visibility. This skill grants: ● A +5 bonus to all Stealth-related checks when acting alone ● Improved ability to blend into environments and avoid detection ● Greater control over when and how you are noticed You are not invisible but unless someone is actively searching, you are rarely seen.

name: Mass Concealment
chain: Concealment → Mass Concealment
description: You extend your mastery of stealth beyond yourself, guiding others in how to move quietly and remain unseen. Through subtle signals, pacing, and environmental awareness, you help groups avoid detection as a unit rather than as individuals. This requires awareness of not just your own actions, but everyone around you, making you a natural leader in stealth-based situations. This skill grants: ● A +3 bonus to all Stealth and disappearance-based checks for allies within your presence ● Improved coordination when moving as a group in stealth scenarios ● Increased success when attempting to hide, reposition, or disengage together Unlike Concealment, this skill focuses on the group your presence makes others harder to detect, even if they lack your experience. Routes & Trade Skills

name: Safer Routes
chain: Safer Routes → Ghost Run
description: You have developed a strong instinct for survival, especially when situations begin to turn against you. You know how to quickly identify escape paths, avoid pursuit, and guide yourself or others out of danger with minimal risk. This isn’t just about running, it's about retreating intelligently and knowing when to disengage before things get worse. Does cost 2 AP This skill grants: ● Advantage on all rolls and checks related to retreating or disengaging ● Improved ability to identify safe paths under pressure ● Reduced risk when escaping from hostile situations When things fall apart, you are the reason people make it out.

name: Ghost Run
chain: Safer Routes → Ghost Run
description: You have mastered the art of moving through spaces as if you were never there to begin with. By committing focus and effort, you can guide others through hidden paths, blind spots, and overlooked routes that avoid even heavy surveillance. This is not just stealth — it is controlled disappearance, turning movement into something nearly untraceable. This skill grants: ● By spending 3 AP, you can give advantage on Stealth checks for yourself and allies for the duration of a scene ● Greatly improved ability to navigate guarded or high-risk areas undetected ● The ability to coordinate full-group stealth operations with high success

name: Black Market Access
chain: None
description: You have established connections within underground trade networks that operate beyond the reach of normal authority. These connections grant you access to goods, information, and opportunities that are not available through standard means. Whether through reputation, favors, or past dealings, you are recognized as someone who can be trusted or at least tolerated within these circles. This skill grants: ● Access to a restricted Black Market shop unavailable to most characters unless a special event ○ It will be a locked channel only unlocked for people with this skill unless stated otherwise ● The ability to purchase rare, stronger, or limited-time items ● A +2 bonus to all checks related to black market dealings and smuggling ○ If you have a Criminal background, this bonus increases to +4, reflecting deeper integration into these networks.

name: Silver Tongue
chain: Silver Tongue→ Master Negotiator
description: You possess a natural gift for persuasion, able to weave words in a way that makes others more receptive to your intentions. Whether through charm, logic, or subtle manipulation, you can guide conversations without others immediately realizing it. This ability doesn’t guarantee success, but it consistently tilts social interactions in your favor when used skillfully. Gain a +1 to the following (other bonuses are included): ● Improves persuasion during conversations and negotiations ● Can de-escalate minor conflicts or tensions ● Slightly increases chances of favorable NPC reactions

name: Master Negotiator
chain: Silver Tongue→ Master Negotiator
description: You are a master of dialogue and deal-making, capable of controlling the pace and structure of high-stakes discussions. Your presence alone shifts the balance of power, forcing others to consider compromise even when they hold the advantage. In critical moments, you can shape outcomes that would otherwise seem inevitable. Once per scene, boost chances for a temporary ceasefire or compromise: ● Gain an advantage roll when rolling for this along with bonuses ● Can broker complex agreements between multiple parties ● Turn unfavorable deals into balanced or advantageous ones

name: Reputation Management
chain: Reputation Management → Political Network
description: You understand that perception is often more important than truth, and you actively work to maintain a favorable image. Through careful wording and timely responses, you can minimize the damage of mistakes or rumors. While not immune to consequences, you are far better at controlling how those consequences are perceived. As long as your character isn’t viewed negatively and up to GM discretion: ● Reduce negative consequences from minor failures or scandals

name: Political Network
chain: Reputation Management → Political Network
description: You have cultivated a wide network of allies, informants, and favors that extends beyond direct interactions. This network allows you to gather information, call in assistance, and influence events without being physically present. Your reputation now precedes you, shaping situations before you even arrive. Requires you to be viewed positively, and you must keep up with an extensive list of allies: ● Call in assistance, information, or minor resources once per scene ○ Must have access to use them for help ● Gain early warnings about threats or opportunities Economic Skills

name: Passive Income
chain: None
description: You maintain small but consistent sources of revenue through side deals, favors, or minor holdings. These earnings require little direct oversight, allowing you to focus on other matters while still benefiting financially. Though modest, this income provides steady support over time. Gain a small amount of currency between scenes: ● If you are working with a NPC that has a shop and after helping at the end of a scene your gm can set up a d20 and based on modifiers and what you did can decide if you have access to gaining profit from them. ● You can only have a max of 5 passive income opportunities at once. Aswell they only give you anywhere from 10-50 currency (rolled by GM too) at the end of the month. ○ It is up to the player to remind staff to give it to them. ○ If missed it cannot be doubled up, but a grace period is allowed if by a couple of days.

name: Investment Making
chain: None
description: You are capable of allocating resources toward opportunities that may yield future returns. While not every investment is guaranteed to succeed, you have a basic sense of risk versus reward. Over time, your ability to grow wealth through planning can show results that can shop anyone. In a scene anyone with this skill can invest into a NPC shop in a mission: ● Spend currency to gain returns after a set number of scenes ○ After investing any money they currently have to wait 3 scenes (RP or Mission) ● Small chance of bonus profit ○ Based on the roll out of the 1d4: ■ 5% ■ 10% ■ 15% ● Minor risk of partial loss ○ Based on the roll out of the 1d4: ■ Complete lost of money

name: Contractional Servitude
chain: None
description: You possess the ability to turn most things into an opportunity, binding captured or surrendered enemies through agreements. Whether through coercion, leverage, or offering them a path to survival, you force them into temporary service under your authority. These contracts are NOT magical, but are reinforced by your reputation, and the circumstances surrounding their capture. While the control is not absolute, most targets will comply out of necessity, fear, or self-interest: ● Convert one captured enemy into a temporary asset ● Asset remains loyal for 3 mission-based scenes ● Limited access to their abilities ○ You will learn their traits (Max of 5 points, but no negative traits beyond a -1) ■ Including Origin traits are allowed ○ Stats ○ Items (cannot have or give their items away) ● When using them in combat they have their own Action, Reaction and Bonus Action. ○ However they can only act Before or After you.

name: Heavy Armaments
chain: Heavy Armaments → Taunt → Dominion Heavy Armaments → Berserker → Sweeping Strike Heavy Armaments → Honed Strike → Mastered Strike Heavy Armaments shows your skill in a heavy weapon discipline.
description: These weapons include: ● Broadswords, bastard swords, and all two-handed blades ● Heavy hafted weapons - battle axes, warhammers, maces ● Heavy polearms - halberds, tridents, pollaxe, glaives, etc. ● Any other marked “Heavy Arms” + Gain a 2% bonus to damage output when using a heavy arm.

name: Taunt
chain: Heavy Armaments → Taunt → Dominion Taunt is the ability to draw the attention of enemies to yourself, and keep you at the forefront of their attention – often distractingly so. The form this ability actually takes is up to the character.
description: + Bonus Action, 1 AP + Adversaries suffer a 10% reaction penalty to ally’s attacks. + Duration: 1 Turn + Only one person in your team may have Taunt active.

name: Dominion
chain: Heavy Armaments → Taunt → Dominion Dominion is taking control of the battle – you are impossible to ignore.
description: + Bonus Action, 2 AP + Adversaries suffer a 30% reaction penalty to ally’s attacks. + Adversaries suffer a 10% reaction penalty to your own attacks. + Duration: 3 Turns + ONLY ONE PERSON – ALLY OR ENEMY – MAY BE EXPRESSING DOMINION AT A TIME. + Dominion can be broken by another’s use of this skill. + This results in a Dominion clash; each user of Dominion factors the following, with the highest result “winning” and keeping the ability. + (HIGHEST PHYS STAT x 1.25) + (LOWEST PHYS STAT)

name: Berserker
chain: Heavy Armaments → Berserker → Sweeping Strike Berserker is exactly what you think it is – for a limited time, focus all of your energy, adrenaline, and focus into a bloodlust to seize control of the fight. Be careful, however, as this is extremely taxing on the body.
description: + Bonus Action, 3 AP + Duration: 3 Turns + Strength is boosted by x 1.10 + Dexterity is boosted by x 1.10 + Stamina is boosted by x 1.10 + Wound severity is IGNORED. + Wounds obtained during this state are more deadly. + For every injury taken, add a second injury one tier lower. + T1 injuries always result in a second T1. + Wounds given during this state are more deadly. + For every injury given, add a second injury one tier lower. + T1 injuries always result in a second T1. + WHEN THIS EFFECT ENDS, THE FOLLOWING MODIFIERS APPLY FOR THE REST OF THE SCENE: + Strength is weakened: STR x 0.8 + Dex is weakened: DEX x 0.8 + Stamina is weakened: STA x 0.8

name: Sweeping Strike
chain: Heavy Armaments → Berserker → Sweeping Strike Sweeping Strike allows the fighter to hit multiple adversaries in one strike, if they are within reach.
description: This ability is used while standing in place – you cannot move in order to widen the range, or to move out of strike range. + Bonus Action, 1 AP This effect strikes TWO enemies when using a standard heavy weapon. This effect strikes THREE enemies when using a heavy polearm. This ability is capable of friendly fire – allies that are within the weapon’s range will also be hit. This ability functions the same as an AOE. It is prudent to ask the GM for a map before using this ability.

name: Honed Strike
chain: Heavy Armaments → Honed Strike → Mastered Strike Honed Strike is a beginner’s technique to place more strength and purpose into a blow. The fighter puts all of their weight into this move, over-extending their muscles. This usually results in injury.
description: + Bonus Action, 1 AP + Output is increased by 20%. + Fighter takes a T1 injury. + Single turn.

name: Mastered Strike
chain: Heavy Armaments → Honed Strike → Mastered Strike
description: Mastered Strike is the more perfected form of honed strike. With experience, any fighter can learn this move without the risk of over-extension or self-harm. + Bonus Action, 1 AP + This ability REPLACES Honed Strike. + Output is increased by 30%. + Fighter takes no injury.

name: Light Armaments
chain: Light Armaments → Crowd Feint → Swashbuckler Light Armaments → Unbound → Disarm Light Armaments → Duelist → Parry Light Armaments shows your skill in a light weapon discipline.
description: These weapons include: ● Rondels, rapiers, dirks, swordbreakers, kukri etc ● Flexible light weapons, including whips and flails ● Light polearms – including the spear, partisan, naginata ● Any other marked “Light Arms” + Gain a 2% bonus to damage output when using a light arm.

name: Crowd Feint
chain: Light Armaments → Crowd Feint → Swashbuckler Crowd Feint is the ability to fall back into a fighting line, or move across the battle in a way that does not draw attention to yourself. It is NOT sneaking, but rather, a way to ensure your opponent does not see you as the biggest thread on the field.
description: The form this ability actually takes is up to the character. + Bonus Action, 1 AP + Adversaries suffer a 10% reaction penalty to your attacks. + Duration: 1 Turn + Only one person in your team may have Crowd Feint active. + This ability requires at least 2 unrestrained allies besides yourself.

name: Swashbuckler
chain: Light Armaments → Crowd Feint → Swashbuckler Swashbuckler allows you to use cunning and daring to move into and out of focus or range at will, needling opponents as they struggle to deal with allies.
description: + Bonus Action, 2 AP + This ability requires at least 2 unrestrained allies besides yourself. + Duration: 3 Turns + Adversaries suffer a 35% reaction penalty to your attacks. + You gain a reaction score boost of x1.05 when reacting. + Stacks with any other on-going reaction modifiers.

name: Unbound
chain: Light Armaments → Unbound → Disarm Unbound is a focused combat technique, combining precision and speed to unleash a flurry of blows and movement. So much energy and concentration is poured into this ability that it can only be kept up for a limited time – afterward, exhaustion and weakness will take over.
description: + Bonus Action, 3 AP + Duration: 3 Turns + Dexterity is boosted by x 1.20 + Attack Speed is boosted by x 1.10 + Wound severity is IGNORED. + You gain one MIRRORED ACTION per turn during this period. + This must be based on a DEX-BASED ATTACK. + This must be directed at the same target as the mirrored action. + Wounds obtained during this state are more deadly. + For every injury taken, add a second injury one tier lower. + T1 injuries always result in a second T1. + WHEN THIS EFFECT ENDS, THE FOLLOWING MODIFIERS APPLY FOR THE REST OF THE SCENE: + Dex is weakened: STR x 0.7 + Stamina is weakened: STA x 0.7 + Attack Speed is weakened: Attack Speed x 0.9 + Reaction Score is weakened; Reaction Score x 0.9

name: Disarm
chain: Light Armaments → Unbound → Disarm Disarm is a swift technique to attempt to ‘swordbreak’ or disarm an opponent. Useless against unarmed opponents.
description: + Bonus Action, 1 AP + ON SUCCESS: + Target drops weapon. + Target may retrieve their weapon using a bonus action if any of the conditions apply later in the scene: + Combat is completed; + Target becomes concealed or hidden; + Target cannot be reasonably blocked from the weapon (i.e, player team’s reaction economy is overcame by target’s allies) + Target is narratively allowed via RP to retrieve it. This is NOT an autohit ability! Disarm “autohits” only when the opponent CANNOT clash or defend. GM must roll for success AFTER the player makes their post, and calculates normal output results; in player-led spars, the person responsible for rolling is the DEFENDER, OR you may submit a request for a GM. Please communicate appropriately. 1d20 + MODIFIERS 14+ ROLL WINS; NAT 20 LANDS FREE T1 ON TOP OF DISARM; CRIT FAIL player takes T1 and cannot use ability again in the scene. Player takes GUN DAMAGE if CRIT FAILing a disarm against a loaded firearm capable of firing. ACCEPTABLE MODIFIERS: ● Any Disarm modifier bestowed by items, traits, or spells ● Luck, relevant Knowledge skill bonuses, or GM-provided situation bonuses ● +1 for every 10 damage that actually lands

name: Duelist
chain: Light Armaments → Duelist → Parry Duelist is a fighting form that requires quick and nimble movements to outpace an opponent.
description: + Bonus Action, 1 AP + Attack speed is increased: Attack Speed x 1.15 + Effect applies for ALL attacks and reactions within turn.

name: Parry
chain: Light Armaments → Duelist → Parry Becoming an experienced Duelist results in learning how to successfully parry attacks, not just block or dodge them.
description: + Bonus Action, 1 AP + This ability REPLACES Duelist. + Attack speed is increased: Attack Speed x 1.20 + Effect applies for ALL attacks and reactions within turn. + PARRY: + Does not work against ranged attacks, guns, or magic. + On reaction, if attack speed is 20% higher than incoming attack’s speed, you may choose to parry rather than dodge. + Parry reflects 50% of physical damage back to the attacker. + If this occurs in Phase 3, attacker can use their next bonus action to defend in their Action phase.

name: Martial Arts
chain: Martial Arts → Offensive Defense → Furious Technique Martial Arts → Trance → Disarm Martial Arts → Brawler → Grappler Martial Arts is your skill with fighting unarmed.
description: The use of any weapon, including ranged weapons or guns being actively wielded, disables this style. You must be HANDS FREE. Offensive wearables are permitted: ● Gloves, mail mitts, and bracers ● Knuckledusters ● Hand Claws + Gain a 4% bonus to damage output when fighting unarmed.

name: Offensive Defense
chain: Martial Arts → Offensive Defense → Furious Technique Offensive Defense means learning to always have a proactive approach to an attacker, no matter if they are armed or not. You meet attacks head-on.
description: The form this ability actually takes is up to the character. + Bonus Action, 1 AP + Duration: 1 Turn + All clashing reaction attacks get a clashing bonus of x1.15. + Does not work on ranged, gun, or magic attacks.

name: Furious Technique
chain: Martial Arts → Offensive Defense → Furious Technique
description: The best defense is a good offense – right? You are ready to meet challenges and show that you will not back down. + Bonus Action, 2 AP + Duration: 3 Turns + All clashing reaction attacks get a clashing bonus of x1.25. + Does not work on ranged, gun, or magic attacks. + You are able to clash at 100% capacity even when doing partial reactions.

name: Trance
chain: Martial Arts → Trance → Disarm Berserker is exactly what you think it is – for a limited time, focus all of your energy, adrenaline, and focus into a bloodlust to seize control of the fight. Be careful, however, as this is extremely taxing on the body.
description: + Bonus Action, 3 AP + Duration: 3 Turns + Strength is boosted by x 1.15 + Dexterity is boosted by x 1.15 + Wound severity is IGNORED. + Wounds obtained during this state are more deadly. + For every injury taken, add a second injury one tier lower. + T1 injuries always result in a second T1. + You are able to more effectively grapple. + Grappling attacks do x1.15 output. + Stacks with any other Grapple bonus. + You are able to more effectively disarm. + Gain +3 to Disarm roll. + WHEN THIS EFFECT ENDS, THE FOLLOWING MODIFIERS APPLY FOR THE REST OF THE SCENE: + Strength is weakened: STR x 0.8 + Dexterity is weakened: DEX x 0.8

name: Disarm
chain: Martial Arts → Trance → Disarm Disarm is a swift technique to attempt to ‘swordbreak’ or disarm an opponent. Useless against unarmed opponents.
description: + Bonus Action, 1 AP + ON SUCCESS: + Target drops weapon. + Target may retrieve their weapon using a bonus action if any of the conditions apply later in the scene: + Combat is completed; + Target becomes concealed or hidden; + Target cannot be reasonably blocked from the weapon (i.e, player team’s reaction economy is overcame by target’s allies) + Target is narratively allowed via RP to retrieve it. This is NOT an autohit ability! Disarm “autohits” only when the opponent CANNOT clash or defend. GM must roll for success AFTER the player makes their post, and calculates normal output results; in player-led spars, the person responsible for rolling is the DEFENDER, OR you may submit a request for a GM. Please communicate appropriately. 1d20 + MODIFIERS 14+ ROLL WINS; NAT 20 LANDS FREE T1 ON TOP OF DISARM; CRIT FAIL player takes T1 and cannot use ability again in the scene. Player takes GUN DAMAGE if CRIT FAILing a disarm against a loaded firearm capable of firing. ACCEPTABLE MODIFIERS: ● Any Disarm modifier bestowed by items, traits, or spells ● Luck, relevant Knowledge skill bonuses, or GM-provided situation bonuses ● +1 for every 10 damage that actually lands

name: Brawler
chain: Martial Arts → Brawler → Grappler Brawlers are scrappers, able to turn their environment itself into a temporary weapon.
description: + Bonus Action, 1 AP + Output has increased by 15%. + Single Turn

name: Grappler
chain: Martial Arts → Brawler → Grappler Grapplers have mastered creative fighting, and are able to subdue opponents more effectively.
description: + Bonus Action, 1 AP + This ability REPLACES Brawler. + Output has increased by 15%. (x1.15) + When a successful grapple is used with this ability, the fighter does not incur the grapple reflex penalty.

name: Form Mastery
chain: Form Mastery allows a character to choose from a pre-existing form of unarmed fighting, or develop their own.
description: + Form Mastery permits the creation of 1 custom passive or active ability. + This ability MUST be reviewed and approved by admin staff. + Once a Form Mastery is developed, it can be taught to other players who have obtained the prerequisites of Form Mastery. Supplementary

name: Simple Ranged
chain: Simple Ranged unlocks the skill required to use basic ranged weapons.
description: These do NOT include any guns. Gun use must be unlocked through the Gunslinger tree. Simple Ranged weapons include: ● Light bows (ex: Recurve, short bow) ● Long Bows ● Crossbows ● Blowdarts ● Slingshots + Able to effectively use “Simple Ranged” weapons in combat.

name: Restoration School
description: Restoration is the school of healing. These spells do not cause damage, they reverse it. Healing always occurs worst to least, and can heal ONE injury at a time. When stabilizing injuries, or healing a T3 or above, you require full action economy and cannot act or react once started. Taking Regeneration grants the following spell capabilities: ● Can heal T1 injuries in 1 Turn ● Can heal T2 injuries in 2 Turns ● Can stabilize DOTs from T3s and below Example Use: Jerry has a T2 and 3 T1 injuries. Tom can sustain a restoration spell and heal all of Jerry’s injuries in 5 turns: T2 (2 Turns) -> T1 (1 Turn) -> T1 (1 Turn) -> T1 (1 Turn)

name: Advanced Restoration
description: Advanced Restoration is the study of healing advanced physical and magical injuries. Healing always occurs worst to least, and can heal ONE injury at a time. When stabilizing injuries, or healing a T4 or above, you require full action economy and cannot act or react once started. Grants the following: ● 2x T1 injuries in 1 Turn ● 1x T2 injury in 1 Turn ● 1x T3 injury in 2 Turns ● Can stabilize DOTs from T4s and below

name: Mastered Restoration
description: Mastered Restoration is the study of healing catastrophic damage quickly, including suspension of the dying. When stabilizing injuries, or healing a T4 or above, you require full reaction economy and cannot react. Grants the following: ● 4x T1 injuries in 1 Turn ● 2x T2 injuries in 1 Turn ● 1x T3 injury in 1 Turn ● 1x T4 injury in 3 Turns ● Can stabilize T5 injuries NOTATION ON T4+ HEALING: This specialization is on stopping catastrophic damage and death. It CANNOT restore, regrow, or reattach missing or severed organs and limbs. Unlike with lower-tier Restoration techniques, using Mastered Restoration does not run the risk of leaving additional scarring or disabilities when stabilizing T4+ injuries.

name: Purification
description: Allows the use of Restoration to be used as an Area of Effect spell, or to purify special conditions such as disease, poison, and DOTs not caused by an injury. These effects must be created as separate, individual spells. AOE Healing comes at the cost of +20% mana per person to both cast and sustain. AOE Healing spells must be their own spells – this effect cannot be applied to single-target healing spells. Remember that with Restoration, the cast cost and the sustain cost are the same. EXAMPLE AOE HEALING COSTS 2 People 3 People 4 People 5 People Restoration 18 mana 21 mana 24 mana 27 mana Adv. Restoration 30 mana 35 mana 40 mana 45 mana M. Restoration 90 mana 105 mana 120 mana 135 mana

name: Abjuration School
description: Taking Abjuration grants the following spell capabilities: ● Can cast defense type spells Defense spells are short-term personal barrier-like spells that can protect against both magical and physical attacks. Defense spells: ● Can only last 1 turn and cannot be sustained ● Can only protect a single target ● Cost less to cast than normal spells (Cost x 0.75) ● Have an effective output when clashing against magic that is x1.25 ● Have an effective output when clashing against physical of x0.95

name: Wards
description: Wards are defensive “trigger” spells, which can be placed on a target or object, sometimes covertly, and then triggered by a specific condition. Wards are unique in that they can be easily combined with other schools to create complex magical effects. Wards are designed to impact the thing that the ward is attached to – they cannot be used to reflect or deal damage to others. Wards are sustained at 1/4th the mana cost instead of half. TYPES OF WARDS TYPE POWER BAND NOTES Simple Wards Any Trigger a defense spell when hit with damage. Can only defend against 75% the maximum output of the power band used. Complex Wards Standard+ Combines a simple ward with a compatible effect from another school.

name: Barriers
description: Barriers are powerful defense spells that can control or protect entire areas. They generally take the shape of a dome or sphere. The size and strength of a barrier is limited by the power band it is cast at. POWER BAND Maximum Size Maximum Health Minimal (5 mana) 5 meters x1.5 Weak (10 mana) 10 meters x1.5 Standard (25 mana) 25 meters x2 Heavy (50 mana) 50 meters x2 Extreme (100 mana) 100 meters x2.5 SUSTAINING BARRIERS Barriers are hard to maintain. Mages cannot move around while sustaining a barrier, leaving them physically vulnerable. They can still cast spells. Barriers have a normal sustain cost (½ mana cost). REPAIRING BARRIERS Barriers can be repaired to full health by using a bonus action. This is not free. The cost to repair is determined by the percentage of the barrier that has been damaged. You must still also pay to sustain the barrier in the same turn. The cost to repair a barrier is 25% of the cost it took to cast it. ( Cost = Mana Cost x 0.25 )

name: Reflection
description: Allows the use of reflection with abjuration spells. Requires knowledge of destruction spells. Reflection can only reflect either magical damage or physical damage, NEVER both. There must be separate spells for each type of reflection. Reflection is Source-intensive, and requires concentration to properly use. To use reflection: ● Spells with reflection cost 20% more to cast. ● You are only able to reflect ONCE per turn. ● You must use a bonus action to successfully reflect. ● You may only reflect back damage that was actually taken by the abjuration spell, and it cannot have fully broken the spell. ● You may reflect back a maximum of 2/3rds of the abjuration spell’s ability to take output. ○ Example: A barrier with 600 maximum health can reflect up to a maximum of 400. ● SAFE OUTPUT LIMITS APPLY. If you reflect back 400 output when you can only handle doing 200 output, you will take 200 output of damage.

name: Alteration School
description: The Alteration school of magic is the study of spells that alter magic to provide changes to physical things. It is not the study of completing changing or creating matter – that belongs to the Transmutation school. Taking Alteration provides the following: ● Unlocks the ability to cast basic Reinforcement spells Reinforcement spells can be used to directly boost the derived physical stats of a target; this level of alteration cannot be used to impact core stats. These include: ● Dodge ● Reaction ● Fortitude ● AP ● Carry Capacity The power that these are reinforced by are determined by the power band of the spell when it is cast. Power Band Mana Cost Dodge, Reaction, & Fortitude AP, Carry Capacity Minimal 5 +10 N/A Weak 10 +30 +1 Standard 25 +50 +2 Heavy 50 +70 +4 Extreme 100 +100 +6

name: Reinforcement
description: Learning Reinforcement provides the following benefits: ● Unlock the ability to cast advanced Reinforcement spells Reinforcement spells from this tier allow you to buff core physical stats. These include: ● Strength ● Dexterity ● Stamina ● NOTE: You CANNOT stack reinforcements of the same type. The power of your reinforcement is determined by the power band you choose to cast at: Power Band Mana Cost STR, DEX, STA Standard 25 +30 Heavy 50 +50 Extreme 100 +75

name: Weakening
description: Learning Weakening grants the ability to cast Weakening spells. Weakening spells can affect any physical stat; however, for Weakening spells to go into effect, you must successfully hit your target – if they fully clash against or dodge your spell, it will have no effect. Power Band Mana STR/DEX/STA Dodge, Reaction, Fortitude AP, Carry Capacity Minimal 5 -10 -20 -1 Weak 10 -20 -40 -2 Standard 25 -50 -75 -3 Heavy 50 -75 -100 -6 Extreme 100 -100 -150 -9

name: Transformation
description: Transformation allows the caster to transform their own body, taking on a new organic form. This may be used to mimic other people, simply change their appearance, or even take on animal-like forms. Spells that transform other people require Telekinetics. Power bands determine the extent of the transformation. Transformations require a large up-front cost, but can be sustained for the standard mana rate, according to the power band used. Please follow the table below when constructing spells. Power Band Initial Cost Sustain Description Minimal 8 3 Able to transform one limb Weak 15 5 Able to transform half a body Standard 38 13 Able to fully transform the body; this is limited to human shapes, and cannot replicate small details, nor change voice or sex. Heavy 75 25 Full body transformation; limited to human shapes. Can replicate small details, and change voice and sex. Extreme 150 50 Full body transformation. No longer limited to humanoid forms. Non-humanoid forms must be mundane and pre-approved within the spell.

name: Mana Form
prereq_raw: T4; Transformation & 3 Magic Schools & KARMA LOCKED
description: A Mana Form is an exceedingly rare ability, requiring access or mastery of raw Source. Mana Forms require 200 mana to cast, but they do not have a sustain cost and may be maintained until they are removed by the caster or until the end of the scene. ● Mana Forms cannot be used on other targets. ● Mana Forms are unique to the caster, and cannot be taught or shared. ● Mana Forms are KARMA LOCKED. This means: ○ You must have pre-approval from staff. ○ You must have a positive Karma record, proving an ability to handle difficult or demanding roleplay and an ability to create and drive plots. ○ You must demonstrate substantial RP to obtain the form. You may create Mana Forms for Forces that you have at T4 or above. ● Mana Forms must take on a physical manifestation (they cannot be metaphysical). ● Mana Forms have staff-approved abilities, bonuses, and weaknesses while they are active.

name: Destruction School
description: The Destruction school studies the practice of offensive, damaging, and harm-causing magics. Learning Destruction: ● Grants the ability to cast Offense spells ● Grants the ability to cast Restraint spells Power Bands dictate the cost and overall power of Destruction spells. Offense spells are standard attack spells. Restraint spells are a spell type that attempts to magically restrain a target. ● Restraint spells themselves do not cause damage. ● To successfully restrain, a restraint spell must successfully hit the target. ● Restrained people: ○ Cannot sustain spells, use magic, or move ○ Can attempt to break out using a bonus action ○ To escape Restraint, follow the same formula as breaking out of a Grapple: ■ (STR + STA + Modifiers) > Restraint Spell Output

name: Offensive Area Magic
description: Learning Offensive Area Magic: ● Unlocks the ability to cast AOE Restraint spells ● Unlocks the ability to cast AOE Destruction spells AOEs must be their own spell. AOEs cost +5% more to cast per additional target.

name: Transmutation School
description: This school unlocks the following spell types: Transmutation, Supplementary - Example spells include summoning a sword, or transforming the metal in a lock to something brittle. Supplementary are “miscellaneous” spells that change the way the world works – for example, a featherlight or gravity spell. Supplementary is often combined with another spell type to create very complex effects. Transmutation and Supplementary spells allow for full creativity; however, all transmute spells must be approved before use. More powerful effects will require higher power bands to cast. Rules ● You must be able to DODGE the attack to transmute it into a different object. ● It must have the same or loosely the similar amount of mass ● You can transmute a spell but not into a different spell, and if the enemies uses a higher spell it won't work. ● If the spell band is the same but their force tier exceeds yours it won't work

name: Telemancy School
description: Learning Telemancy unlocks the following: ● Able to cast Communication spells ● Able to cast Mark spells Mark Spells ● Marks are a generic, minimal form of Source. ○ Marks have utility across multiple Schools; they can be used as a “homing beacon” for very long distance spells, tracking, and many other tactical uses. ● Marks are all touch-based spells to apply. ● Marks are like fingerprints, and the Source used to make them may fade over time if they are not actively maintained. ○ This time is measured in IRP days; usually 1 day per power band used to create them. (Minimal = 1 day; Extreme = 5 days) Communication Spells Communication magic allows the caster to project auditory or visual messages across distances. ● The maximum distance a communication spell can be used is based on AFFINITY: ○ Affinity x 0.01 = Maximum distance in meters ● An existing Mark spell, so long as the caster has knowledge of it, can circumvent the distance limitations. Communication Power Bands Casting different kinds of communication spells requires different power bands. Please refer to the table below for guidelines. Power Band Communication Limitations Minimal Open broadcast spells; everyone in range can hear. Cannot use marks. Weak One-way audio spells. Recipient must cast their own communication spell to reply. Standard Two-way audio spells. Caster must sustain this spell, but allows recipient to reply freely. Heavy Two-way audio-visual spells. Caster must sustain this spell, but allows recipient to reply freely. Extreme Able to do targeted broadcasts to multiple people at once, even if spread out. These are ‘private’ to the broadcast recipients.

name: Illusion
description: Learning Telemancy unlocks the following: ● Able to cast Illusion spells Illusion is a practice taught under Telemancy that focuses on the transmission of thought-altering spells rather than simple communication. Illusions can trick or deceive. When combined with another spell discipline, this allows for the creation of very complex and unique spells. The scope and power of an illusion is dependent on its power band. All illusion spells must be reviewed and approved before they can be used.

name: Telekinetics
description: Telekinetics is a complex study of Source, that permits the use of Telemancy “at a distance.” Unlocking Telekinetics: ● Enables more corporeal effects for all Telemancy and Illusion spells An example of a very simple telekinetic spell is straightforward telekinesis – moving or affecting objects at a distance without the use of a Force, and without transmuting them. However, telekinetics can be used for much more than this. Combined with Illusion, it allows for “physical” manifestations of illusions; combined with other schools, it can enable complex remote abilities or extend the range of spells. Telekinetic spells must be reviewed and approved before they can be used.

name: Runic Study
description: Runic Study is a purely academic school of magic. It is the direct study of raw Source. Runic Study is sometimes used to directly work with raw Source. Unlocking Runic Study: ● Enables the creation of Runes Magic Skills Beyond forces and schools, there are a number of magic-related skills. Each magic skill has an associated TIER. ● Skill Tiers DO NOT depend on Forces. ● Skill Tiers are ONLY used to determine if a character can learn the skill at all (i.e, what is available to everyone, or all-rounders)

name: Mana Skin
description: Mana Skin → Reflexive Mana Skin Mana Skin is a very simple but often undertrained skill; the technique allows a person to control their body’s own mana to serve as a temporary shield against hostile environments or magical effects. ACTIVE SKILL This spell costs 1 AP each time it is used to reduce damage from attacks. Requires a bonus action to activate. + Drains 2% of mana per turn to maintain. + Immunity to weak Source wells + While active, can ‘absorb’ magical attacks, reducing incoming magical damage by 10%. + When using Mana Skin to reduce damage, this costs 1 AP.

name: Reflexive Mana Skin
description: Mana Skin → Reflexive Mana Skin Reflexive Mana Skin is an advanced skill honed by steady practice with using mana skin. The technique allows a person to instinctively use their own body’s mana to serve as a temporary shield against hostile environments, magical effects, and attacks – however concentrating mana in this way will shock the physical body. Taking Reflexive Mana Skin: ● Changes the way active use of Mana Skin works: ○ Mana Skin will now also reduce physical damage, not just magical. ○ Mana Skin will now guard against Strong Source wells Passive Use of Reflexive Mana Skin: ● Reflexive Mana Skin allows Mana Skin to be used passively, at an increased cost. ● When used passively, Mana Skin is automatically triggered to reduce damage so long as a bonus action and at least 1 AP is available, unless the character chooses to suppress the skill. ○ Characters may choose to consume the bonus action of the following turn if they do not have a bonus action available in the current turn. + When this skill is activated, it can ‘absorb’ magical attacks or physical attacks. + Any damage is reduced by 15%. + This requires use of a bonus action and 1 AP. + Adds the following Penalty, which lasts 1 Turn: + -15% Strength, Dexterity, & Stamina

name: Mana Sensing
description: Mana Sensing → Mana Tracking Mana Sensing is a concentration skill, learned by using one’s own mana circuits to detect disturbances or changes in mana further away. With mana sensing, someone can detect the presence of wards, catalysts, spells, and even people or creatures with significant mana. ACTIVE SKILL Requires a free action to activate. + Drains 2% of mana per turn to maintain. + Allows detection of place and movement of wards, catalysts, spells, or living things with significant mana. + DISTANCE: Mana * 0.20, in meters DETECTION OF LIVING THINGS: Any living with greater than 100 remaining mana Excludes anything shielded with Mana Concealment

name: Mana Tracking
description: Mana Sensing → Mana Tracking Mana Tracking is an advanced version of Mana Sensing, allowing a mage to detect traces of mana even if the source has moved or is no longer around. It is a much more keen sense, allowing for a wider sensory range and detecting much lower levels of Source. ACTIVE SKILL Requires a free action to activate. + Drains 2% of mana per turn to maintain. + Allows detection of place and movement of wards, catalysts, spells, or living things with significant mana. + DISTANCE: Mana * 0.40, in meters DETECTION OF LIVING THINGS: Any living thing with mana + MANA CONCEALMENT DETECTION: + Can detect concealed mana if Affinity x 0.9 > Hidden Affinity Mana Concealment skills are not yet implemented for players, but may be used by GMs.

name: Overcharge
chain: Overcharge → Mastered Overcharge Overcharge allows a mage to “overcharge” a spell in order to make an overwhelming attack, or defend against one.
description: Overcharging requires the use of a bonus action and 1 AP. Overcharging an ACTION: ● At the cost of x1.5 mana, do +35% more spell damage. ● SAFE OUTPUT LIMITS ARE IN PLAY. Overcharging a REACTION: ● You may do x2 times normal output when overcharging a purely defensive spell. ● There is no additional mana cost for this. ● SAFE OUTPUT LIMITS ARE IGNORED. ● Overcharged barriers are unstable and will immediately break after they are used to defend.

name: Mastered Overcharge
chain: Overcharge → Mastered Overcharge
description: Mastered Overcharge allows a mage to “overcharge” a spell in order to make an overwhelming attack, or defend against one. Overcharging requires the use of a bonus action and 1 AP. Overcharging an ACTION: ● Do +35% more spell damage. ● This may only be done once per turn. ● SAFE OUTPUT LIMITS ARE IN PLAY. Overcharging a REACTION: ● You may do x2.5 times normal output when overcharging a purely defensive spell. ● SAFE OUTPUT LIMITS ARE IGNORED. ● Overcharged barriers are unstable and will immediately break after they are used to defend.

name: Compounding
chain: None Compounding involves combining a spell with another mage to create a unique or overwhelming effect. Compounding will require a bonus action to trigger.

name: Regeneration
chain: Regeneration → Focused Regeneration Regeneration is a passive skill that allows a mage to regenerate mana over time.
description: Mana regeneration occurs at the end of a turn, during the tick. Mages gain +10 mana per turn.

name: Focused Regeneration
chain: Regeneration → Focused Regeneration Focused Regeneration is a passive skill that allows a mage to regenerate mana over time.
description: Mana regeneration occurs at the end of a turn, during the tick. Mages gain +20 mana per turn.

name: Enchantment
chain: None Enchantment allows for the creation of charged catalysts, or the alteration, fortification, or recharging of catalysts. This is a crafting skill, which uses catalyst materials to create a component used in the creation of magical items.

name: Fortified Circuits
chain: None Fortified Circuits allows the caster to handle more output without taking damage. Adds a permanent passive modifier to Fortitude, when casting magical spells. New Fortitude = Fortitude + (Affinity x 0.15)

name: Utility Beginner
description: The Utility Path is the path that is the benchmark to everything. No matter what path you choose to learn after everything comes from here. This gives you access to creating non combat and mundane items. You do not gain a discount for creating the item however now when it comes to rolls using the item you do gain a permanent +1. When you use items you created. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ○ Must label if its a small, medium or large item ● Item does not have a boosted durability ● Can create a small bag to increase your inventory by 2 points. Example Use: Ben created a hammer, it has a +1 to it for rolls. The hammer cannot be used for combat. However if Ben was making a roll to fix a door along with other modifiers it would have the +1 added to it.

name: Utility Practitioner
description: This gives you access to creating non combat and mundane items. You now have a 5% discount when making the item. Along with the boost to rolls becoming a permanent +2 & items have their durability increased by 5%. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ○ Must label if its a small, medium or large item ● The item does gain a boost in durability by 5% along with a 5% discount when creating it. ● Can create a medium bag to increase your inventory by 3 points. Example Use: Ben created a hammer, it has a +2 to it for rolls. The hammer cannot be used for combat. However if Ben was making a roll to fix a door along with other modifiers it would have the +2 added to it.

name: Utility Expert
description: This gives you access to creating non combat and mundane items. You now have a 10% discount when making the item. Along with the boost to rolls becoming a permanent +3 & items have their durability increased by 10%. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ○ Must label if its a small, medium or large item ● The item does gain a boost in durability by 10% along with a 10% discount when creating it. ● Can create a Large bag to increase your inventory by 5 points. Example Use: Ben created a hammer, it has a +3 to it for rolls. The hammer cannot be used for combat. However if Ben was making a roll to fix a door along with other modifiers it would have the +3 added to it. Smithing Rules for Smithing In a week's time you are only allowed a limited amount of items to be created in a week. Based on the tier you are in is the amount and if you level up the skill it will change the max but if you already for example used 3 in a week and now have a max of 5 you only get 2 more. When making items you must make the sub, pay the amount to make it and make a post that is 1 paragraph [100 words minimum].

name: Smithing Rookie
description: The Smithing Path, unlike the utility path, deals with creating armor and weapons for specific combat. However to gain access to this you must get the first tier of crafting. This gives you access to creating combat weapons and armor. You do not gain a discount for creating the item. For weapons you gain a permanent 2% boost to your weapons already added modifier and for armor you gain a permanent 2% boost to your armor's already added modifier. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ○ Must label if its a small, medium or large item ● Weapon/Armor gains an added 2% to their set modifier boost ● Item does not have a boosted durability Example Use: Sarah, a regular greatsword, gives a +2% to her strength based attacks, however Bently, a forgeborn created greatsword, gives a +4% to his strength based attacks.

name: Smithing Novice
description: This gives you access to creating combat weapons and armor. You gain a 5% discount for creating the item along with a 5% boost to their durability. For weapons you gain a permanent 3% boost to your weapons already added modifier and for armor you gain a permanent 3% boost to your armor's already added modifier. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ○ Must label if its a small, medium or large item ● Weapon/Armor gains an added 3% to their set modifier boost ● The item does gain a boost in durability by 5% along with a 5% discount when creating it. Example Use: Sarah, a regular greatsword, gives a +2% to her strength based attacks, however Bently, a forgeborn created greatsword, gives a +5% to his strength based attacks.

name: Smithing Extradinaire
description: This gives you access to creating combat weapons and armor. You gain a 10% discount for creating the item along with a 10% boost to durability. For weapons you gain a permanent 4% boost to your weapons already added modifier and for armor you gain a permanent 4% boost to your armor's already added modifier. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ○ Must label if its a small, medium or large item ● Weapon/Armor gains an added 4% to their set modifier boost ● The item does gain a boost in durability by 10% along with a 10% discount when creating it. Example Use: Sarah, a regular greatsword, gives a +2% to her strength based attacks, however Bently, a forgeborn created greatsword, gives a +5% to his strength based attacks. Snares Rules for Snare Making In a week's time you are only allowed a limited amount of items to be created in a week. Based on the tier you are in is the amount and if you level up the skill it will change the max but if you already for example used 3 in a week and now have a max of 5 you only get 2 more. When making items you must make the sub, pay the amount to make it and make a post that is 1 paragraph [100 words minimum]. List of traps

name: Snare Trainee
description: The Snare Path, unlike the utility path, deals with miniature traps, to be used before combat starts to surprise unlikely people. However to gain access to this you must get the first tier of crafting. This gives you access to creating miniature traps. Snare Trainee you get access to 2 out of 10 options for traps. You don’t get a discount on making them however one places them ready. Traps are held in a pack of 2, but only count as 1 for inventory. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ● Choose 2 out of 10 traps you have access to (can’t change)

name: Snare Apprentice
description: This gives you access to creating more miniature traps and can now add effects from the chemical path. Snare Apprentice can now have 4 of 10 options for traps. You get a 5% discount when making traps or a 10% when using a chemical solvent from the Chemical Path. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ● Choose 4 out of 10 traps you have access to ○ Can decide on a new 4 but can’t change after ● You get 5% discount or a 10% discount if mixed in with Chemical path ○ It will use the potion up if you do decide to.

name: Snare Master
description: This gives you access to creating more miniature traps and with your skill now create more destructive ones using the Demolition path. Snare Master can now have 6 of 10 options for traps. You still have the 5% discount when making traps, the 10% when using a chemical solvent from the Chemical Path and now 15% for explosives using Demolition Path. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ● Choose 6 out of 10 traps you have access to ○ Can decide on a new 6 but can’t change after ● You get 5% discount, 10% discount if mixed in with Chemical path, lastly 15% discount for explosives added with Demolition Path ○ It will use the potion up if you do decide to. ○ It will use the explosive if you do decide to. Demolition Rules for Demolition Making In a week's time you are only allowed a limited amount of items to be created in a week. Based on the tier you are in is the amount and if you level up the skill it will change the max but if you already for example used 3 in a week and now have a max of 5 you only get 2 more. When making items you must make the sub, pay the amount to make it and make a post that is 1 paragraph [100 words minimum]. List of Effects [Demolition]

name: Demolition Learner
description: The Demolition Path is the most destructive and damaging of all paths. However to gain access to this you must get the first tier of crafting. This gives you access to creating bombs and explosives. These can be non-damaging or damaging ones; it depends on the effect you choose to use. At this rank you only have access to simple effects, however only a single effect per grenade/explosive. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ● Only 1 Simple Effect per grenade

name: Demolition Trainee
description: At this rank you only have access to making grenades with 2 simple effects in a singular grenade or a singular complex effect per grenade/explosive. You get a 5% discount when making explosives or a 10% when using a chemical solvent from the Chemical Path. Grenades/Explosives are held in a pack of 2, but only count as 1 for inventory. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ● Only 2 Simple Effect per grenade or 1 Complex Effect per grenade ● You get 5% discount or a 10% discount if mixed in with Chemical path ○ It will use the potion up if you do decide to.

name: Demolition Specialist
description: At this rank you only have access to making grenades with 4 simple effects in a singular grenade or 2 complex effects per grenade/explosive. You get a 5% discount when making explosives or a 10% when using a chemical solvent from the Chemical Path, as well a 15% when adding a trap with the grenade/explosive. Grenades/Explosives are held in a pack of 2, but only count as 1 for inventory. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ● 4 Simple Effect per grenade, 2 Complex Effect per grenade or 2 simple and 1 complex effect in per grenade ● You get 5% discount, 10% discount if mixed in with Chemical path, lastly 15% discount for traps added with Snare Path Path ○ It will use the potion up if you do decide to. ○ It will use the trap if you do decide to. Chemistry Rules for Chemists In a week's time you are only allowed a limited amount of items to be created in a week. Based on the tier you are in is the amount and if you level up the skill it will change the max but if you already for example used 3 in a week and now have a max of 5 you only get 2 more. When making items you must make the sub, pay the amount to make it and make a post that is 1 paragraph [100 words minimum]. List of Effects [Chemical]

name: Chemical Assistant
description: The Chemical Path is the inquisitive and creative of all paths. However to gain access to this you must get the first tier of crafting. This gives you access to creating solvents, potions and poisons. These can be non-damaging or damaging ones; it depends on the effect you choose to use as well the application. At this rank you only have access to simple effects, however only a single effect per elixir. Elixirs are held in a pack of 3, but only count as 1 for inventory. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ● Only 1 Simple Effect per grenade

name: Chemical Analyst
description: At this rank you only have access to making elixirs with 2 simple effects in a singular elixir or a singular complex effect per elixir. You get a 5% discount when making elixirs or a 10% when using a trap from the Snare Path. Elixirs are held in a pack of 3, but only count as 1 for inventory. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ● Only 2 Simple Effect per elixir or 1 Complex Effect per elixir ● You get 5% discount or a 10% discount if mixed in with Snare path ○ It will use the trap up if you do decide to.

name: Chemical Scientist
description: At this rank you only have access to making elixirs with 2 simple effects in a singular elixir or a singular complex effect per elixir. You get a 5% discount when making elixirs, 10% when using a trap from the Snare Path and 15% when adding explosives from the Demolition Path. Elixirs are held in a pack of 3, but only count as 1 for inventory. Rules to Follow: ● Items must be cataloged in your Player Journal, for GM reference ● 4 Simple Effect per elixir, 2 Complex Effect per elixir or 2 simple and 1 complex effect in per elixir ● You get 5% discount, 10% discount if mixed in with Snare path, lastly 15% discount for traps added with Demolition Path Path ○ It will use the trap up if you do decide to. ○ It will use the explosive if you do decide to. Forgecraft Skills

name: Tool Proficiency
chain: None Tool Proficiency, is hands-on experience with tools, allowing a character with this skill to work efficiently, safely and correctly even under poor condition or pressure. The character instinctively selects the right tool and aids accidents as long as it’s choosing or knowing what is the correct tool.
description: When doing an action or attempting to do an action where you must understand a tool's use and how to use it you get a modifier boost. + Modifier Boost as follows: - +2 to all rolls dealing with tools and having to know how their used or what they are - Luck modifier

name: Lockpicking
chain: Lockpicking → Lock Mechanism Lockpicking, the ability to bypass mechanical locks, latches and security mechanisms. Effects
description: When doing an action or attempting to do an action where you must use a lockpick for either locking or unlocking, you receive a lockpicking modifier. + Modifier Boost as follows: - +2 to all rolls dealing with lockpicking - Reflexes modifier - Luck modifier

name: Field Stabilizer
chain: N/A
description: Using their instinct for repairs and their eye for what’s “broken,” the user quickly identifies the weak point in an enemy’s stance, armor, or technique and calls it out like a battlefield tactician. By directing an ally where to strike for maximum impact, the chosen ally gains a temporary boost to their clash output for one clash. USING THIS SKILL CONSUMES 1 AP & A Bonus Action + When this skill is activated, it boost a chosen player besides the person with the skill better block an attack + Incoming attacks marked by the user keep their original output but the ally’s attempting to block the attack gain a boost. + The ally’s attack gains a 10% boost to clashing for 1 turn. -
