name: Integrity Reading
chain: N/A Integrity Reading, the user’s trained eye for repairs lets them “read” how intact the enemy's armor or weapon is. By recognizing stress lines, weak materials, and unstable structure, the user can either gain a clear idea of the tools durability. All they must do is grab the item or armor and it is gained.

name: Lock Mechanism
chain: Lockpicking → Lock Mechanism Must have a Lock Kit to use this skill. Lock Mechanism: Allows the user to treat locks as a mechanical system they can analyze and bypass.
description: The user can reverse their tinkering knowledge to perform lockpicking and strengthen a lock to be harder to open. When using this skill on an open lock they can not only close it but also make it harder to open. Effects When doing an action or attempting to do an action where you must use a lockpick the lock affected will have to roll against the user with their modifiers added. [If someone rolls a nat 1, regardless of number they lose and vice versa for nat 20] -

name: Beginning Repairman
chain: Beginning Repairman → Intermediate Repairman Beginner Repairman – The user understands basic maintenance and restoration, able to fix damaged items through careful effort, replacement parts, and simple reinforcement. When using a Repair Kit, the user may repair an item up to 1/3 of its total value, but doing so consumes the entire
description: Post Requirement: When repairing an item, the user must make 2 paragraphs, each with a minimum of 100 words, per item repaired. -

name: Item Modification
chain: N/A
description: The Forgeborn enhances an item by reinforcing its structure, balance, and function through careful adjustments and precise Forgecraft. Once activated, the chosen item gains a 5% stat increase to one of the following stats: Strength, Dexterity, or Stamina. This boost can be activated for 5 turns, after which the item returns to normal. You as well now gain the ability to boost use Knowledge skills, specifically noted to have special abilities tied to them with forgeborn. When creating an item and wanting to modify it with this let staff know, Post Requirement: To activate this skill, the user must complete a scene with the chosen person lasting at least 5 posts total. Across those 5 posts, both the Forgeborn and the other participant must write 3 paragraphs per post, with a minimum of 100 words per paragraph.

name: Intermediate Repairman
chain: Beginning Repairman → Intermediate Repairman Intermediate Repairman – The user has developed stronger technique and efficiency, allowing them to restore items with less waste and better precision. When using a Repair Kit:
description: ● Repair an item up to 1/2 of its total value using the entire kit ● Repair an item up to 1/3 of its total value while allowing the kit to be used twice before it is fully consumed. ○ Must document that repair kit Post Requirement: When repairing an item, the user must make 3 paragraphs, each with a minimum of 100 words, per item repaired. -

name: Radio Countermeasure
chain: Beginning Repairman → Intermediate Repairman
description: + 2 AP + Bonus Action The Forgeborn constructs a compact personal radio system designed for short-range, secure communication. Through this skill, the user may create up to 3 radios for group use. These radios only function properly when kept within 5 meters of each other, ensuring the signal stays stable and difficult to intercept. In addition, the Forgeborn’s master radio can emit a disruptive counter-signal that directly clashes against communication-based spells. When used this way, the Forgeborn forces a raw signal battle between both sides: 1d20 vs 1d20, with no modifiers or boost effects allowed.

name: Field Stabilizer Extraordinaire
chain: Field Stabilizer → Field Stabilizer Extraordinaire
description: With sharper repair instincts and a deeper understanding of what’s about to “break” in combat, the user rapidly reads the enemy’s weak points and calls out precise strike zones like a battlefield tactician. The user may either grant a strong clash output boost to one chosen ally for one clash, or grant a weaker clash output boost to multiple allies for one clash. USING THIS SKILL CONSUMES 1 AP + When this skill is activated, it boost a chosen player or players besides the person with the skill better block an attack + Incoming attacks marked by the user keep their original output but the allies attempting to block the attack gain a boost. + If a single ally is chosen they gain a 25% boost to clashing for 1 turn. + If two allies are chosen they gain a 15% boost to clashing, each for 1 turn.

name: Master Repairman
chain: Master Repairman – The user’s repair skill reaches professional mastery, allowing them to restore even heavily damaged items with near-perfect results. When using a Repair Kit, the user may choose one of the following options:
description: ● Repair an item to full value, consuming the entire kit, ● Repair an item up to 1/2 of its total value, and the kit may be used twice ○ Must document that repair kit ● Repair an item up to 1/3 of its total value, and the kit may be used three times. ○ Must document that repair kit Post Requirement: When repairing an item, the user must make 4 paragraphs, each with a minimum of 100 words, per item repaired. -

name: Magical Repairman
chain: Magical Repair – The Forgeborn applies Forgecraft directly to enchanted and magically infused items, repairing not only the physical structure, but also the damaged magic circuits, bindings, and rune integrity within. This skill follows the same repair scaling as Master Repairman, but may only be used on magical items.
description: Effect Options (Choose One): ● Repair a magical item to full value, consuming the entire Repair Kit ● Repair a magical item up to 1/2 of its total value, and the kit may be used twice ○ Must document that repair kit ● Repair a magical item up to 1/3 of its total value, and the kit may be used three times ○ Must document that repair kit Post Requirement: ● When repairing a magical item, the user must make 4 paragraphs, each with a minimum of 100 words, per item repaired. -

name: Magical Modification
chain: Magical Modification – The Forgeborn temporarily enhances a magical item by reinforcing its enchantment structure, stabilizing its rune flow, and improving how the item channels power into its wielder. This skill follows the same rules as Item Modification, but may only be used on magical items.
description: Effect: The chosen magical item gains a 10% stat increase to the following stat: Magical Affinity. The boost lasts for 5 turns. Activation Requirement: ● To activate this skill, the user must complete a scene with another person lasting at least 5 posts total. Across those 5 posts, both the Forgeborn and the other participant must write 3 paragraphs per post, with a minimum of 100 words per paragraph. ● Must have a someone with Mana Circuits and access to the Skill Enchantnent. - Appendices: Crafting Rules Understanding The Snare Path ● The Snare Path focuses on portable battlefield control through mechanical traps, snares, and triggered devices. A Forgeborn’s rank within this path determines how many trap designs they have access to craft, how many they may carry at once, and the overall complexity and effectiveness of those traps. Higher ranks unlock more advanced mechanisms and ways to use them. ● The specific traps available to craft and how they function depend on the rank chosen within the Snare Path, meaning progression directly increases both versatility and tactical control. The 10 types of traps ● 1. Snapwire Trap ○ A near-invisible wire connected to a spring-loaded mechanism. When tripped, it releases a snapping bar or blunt striker aimed at the legs, potentially knocking the target off balance or interrupting movement. ● 2. Pressure Mine ○ A compact plate device that activates when stepped on. It can be configured for concussive force, smoke discharge, or flash output depending on preparation. ■ Can be used with Demolition Path ● 3. Caltrop Disperser ○ A small throwable capsule that bursts on impact, scattering sharpened spikes across the ground. Enemies moving through the area risk slowed movement or minor injury. ● 4. Net Launcher Trap ○ Triggered by pressure or tripwire, this trap fires a weighted net that entangles a target’s limbs. The net restricts movement until removed or cut free. ● 5. Flashburst Capsule ○ When triggered, this device releases a blinding flash paired with a loud concussive bang. Targets within range may suffer temporary disorientation. ● 6. Adhesive Snare ○ A ground-placed trap that releases a fast-hardening alchemical resin around the feet of whoever activates it. This can root or significantly slow the target for a short duration. ■ Can be used with Chemical Path ● 7. Shock Pad ○ A thin concealed plate that delivers a brief electrical discharge when stepped on. Designed to stagger or momentarily stun rather than deal heavy damage. ● 8. Collapsing Anchor Trap ○ Upon activation, anchored cables snap inward from two directions, pulling tight around a target’s legs or midsection. Often used to restrain or knock someone prone. ● 9. Smoke Veil Canister ○ Triggered by motion or remotely activated, this device releases a dense cloud of smoke or airborne based chemical to either obscure vision or release chemical agents. ■ Can be used with Chemical Path ● 10. Signal Alarm Node ○ A small detection device that emits a sharp audible alarm or sends a signal to the Forgeborn when disturbed. Primarily used for perimeter security but can have explosives added to make it known it was tripped and people may be injured. ■ Can be used with Demolition Path Understanding The Demolition Path ● The Demolition Path focuses on controlled destruction, concussive force, and calculated explosive engineering. Unlike the Snare Path, which emphasizes restraint and battlefield control, Demolition specialists excel at breaking structures. ● A Forgeborn’s rank within the Demolition Path determines the effects that can be added to the grenade. Along with how many effects or addition that can be properly added. Simple Effects ● 1. TBA Complex Effects ● 1. TBA Understanding The Chemical Path ● The Chemical Path focuses on alchemical compounds, battlefield substances, and reactive mixtures designed to alter the flow of combat. Rather than relying purely on force or restraint, Chemical specialists manipulate reactions burning, freezing, dissolving, obscuring, or enhancing through carefully prepared formulas. ● A Forgeborn’s rank within the Chemical Path determines the potency, stability, and versatility of the compounds they can create. Along with how many effects or additions that can be properly added. Simple Effects ● 1. TBA Complex Effects ● 1. TBA

name: Wheelgun Familiarity (T1)
chain: Wheelgun Familiarity → Quickdraw Revolver → Revolver Virtuoso
description: You possess foundational training with revolvers, understanding their mechanics, weight, recoil, and firing rhythm. Through practice, you have learned how to properly maintain the weapon, manage its cylinder, and fire with steady control even under pressure. While you may not yet possess the refined speed of veteran gunslingers, you are comfortable enough with revolvers that they feel natural in your hand rather than awkward or unfamiliar. This familiarity grants you improved accuracy and stability when firing revolvers, especially in situations where quick reactions are required. Now you gain access to T1, Revolver specific, Gun Skills and T1 revolvers.

name: Quickdraw Revolver (T2)
chain: Wheelgun Familiarity → Quickdraw Revolver → Revolver Virtuoso
description: At this stage, your revolver handling has evolved into a fluid discipline. Drawing your weapon, aligning your sights, and firing become part of a seamless motion that can be executed in the blink of an eye. Through countless repetitions, your body has learned the natural rhythm of the revolver, allowing you to react faster than most shooters in tense situations. This training improves your ability to draw and ready revolvers quickly while maintaining respectable accuracy. Shots fired immediately after drawing suffer fewer penalties, and your speed in sudden confrontations such as duels or ambushes becomes noticeably sharper. Now you gain access to T2, Revolver specific, Gun Skills and T2 revolvers.

name: Revolver Virtuoso (T3)
chain: Wheelgun Familiarity → Quickdraw Revolver → Revolver Virtuoso
description: Your mastery of revolvers has reached an exceptional level, transforming the weapon into an extension of your own instincts. Every movement from cocking the hammer to squeezing the trigger feels effortless. Experienced shooters can recognize the confidence in your technique. This mastery significantly improves your revolver accuracy, recoil control, and shot precision. You are capable of making more advanced shots, such as targeting smaller areas or maintaining accuracy during rapid firing. Handling multiple revolvers becomes far more manageable due to your exceptional control and understanding of the weapon. Now you gain access to T3, Revolver specific, Gun Skills and T3 revolvers.

name: Rifle Training (T1)
chain: Rifle Training → Sharpshooter Discipline → Deadeye Marksman
description: You have received proper instruction in the fundamentals of rifle marksmanship, including posture, breathing control, trigger discipline, and sight alignment. Unlike the chaotic firing style often seen with sidearms, rifle usage rewards patience and steady focus. Through your training, you have developed the basic skills required to reliably place shots at medium distances. This level of proficiency improves your accuracy when using rifles and reduces penalties when firing from moderate range. Your understanding of stable shooting positions and careful aim allows you to perform better when taking deliberate shots rather than rushing them. Now you gain access to T1, Rifle specific, Gun Skills and T1 rifles.

name: Sharpshooter Discipline (T2)
chain: Rifle Training → Sharpshooter Discipline → Deadeye Marksman
description: Your training has deepened into a disciplined and patient style of marksmanship. You have learned to regulate your breathing, steady your posture, and read subtle environmental factors that influence your shot. Whether lying prone, crouched behind cover, or positioned on high ground, you can maintain a level of composure that allows you to land difficult shots. This discipline improves your long-range accuracy and reduces penalties associated with distant targets. Carefully aimed shots become more reliable, and you gain greater success when attempting precision strikes on specific areas or vulnerable points. Now you gain access to T2, Rifle specific, Gun Skills and T2 rifles.

name: Deadeye Marksman (T3)
chain: Rifle Training → Sharpshooter Discipline → Deadeye Marksman
description: You have reached an elite level of rifle mastery that few marksmen ever achieve. Your experience allows you to judge distance, movement, and timing with remarkable clarity. Even under pressure, you maintain the calm focus required to line up and deliver exceptional shots. This mastery greatly enhances your effectiveness at long distances, allowing you to strike targets that most shooters would struggle to hit. Difficult shooting conditions such as elevation changes, distant movement, or awkward positioning are far less disruptive to your accuracy due to your refined skill. Now you gain access to T3, Rifle specific, Gun Skills and T3 rifles.

name: Scattergun Familiarity (T1)
chain: Scattergun Familiarity → Close-Quarters Shooter → Breacher Specialist
description: You have learned the basic handling and practical use of shotguns, understanding both their immense close-range power and their unique firing spread. This training includes recoil management, effective aiming at short distances, and safe handling in confined environments. While shotguns can be unwieldy for inexperienced users, you have developed enough familiarity to wield them with confidence. Your training improves your control when firing shotguns and reduces penalties in close-range engagements. You are also better able to anticipate pellet spread and adjust your aim accordingly, making your shots more reliable within their intended range Now you gain access to T1, Shotgun specific, Gun Skills and T1 shotguns.

name: Close-Quarters Shooter (T2)
chain: Scattergun Familiarity → Close-Quarters Shooter → Breacher Specialist
description: Through continued experience, you have developed strong proficiency with shotguns in tight environments such as buildings, narrow streets, or enclosed battlefields. You understand how to maneuver the weapon quickly, react to sudden threats, and take advantage of the shotgun’s devastating power at short distances. Your awareness and positioning allow you to make the most of its wide spread. This level of expertise improves your effectiveness in close-quarters combat and reduces the difficulty of firing in cramped spaces. You are better able to control the weapon under pressure and react quickly to enemies that suddenly appear within close range. Now you gain access to T2, Shotgun specific, Gun Skills and T2 shotguns.

name: Breacher Specialist (T3)
chain: Scattergun Familiarity → Close-Quarters Shooter → Breacher Specialist
description: You have mastered the brutal efficiency of shotgun combat in controlled engagements. Your skill allows you to use the weapon not only against enemies, but also as a tool for breaching barriers, clearing rooms, and overwhelming opponents before they can react. Years of experience have taught you exactly when and where to apply the shotgun’s overwhelming force. Your mastery increases the impact and reliability of shotgun blasts at close range. You are more capable of clearing obstacles, breaking doors, and dominating short-distance combat situations where the shotgun’s destructive potential is at its peak. Now you gain access to T3, Shotgun specific, Gun Skills and T3 shotguns. Combat Specialization

name: Master Shot Techniques (T3)
chain: Basic Shot Techniques → Adv. Shot Techniques →Master Shot Techniques
description: This skill path is NOT released. Please do not take this skill until updated. Guncraft Skills

name: Fast Hands (T0)
description: You are exceptionally quick with weapon handling and ammunition management. You may swap the ammunition type in a firearm as a free action instead of using a standard action. The muscle memory and quickdraw efficiency developed through constant use of firearms. You may also swap to a different firearm using a free action as well This ability only applies to firearms you are currently wielding.

name: Covered Shot (T1)
description: You are skilled at fighting from defensive positions and making use of the environment. When you are actively using cover such as walls, barricades, crates, or terrain, you gain a situational bonus to dodge or evade checks against attacks coming from directions that the cover protects. This bonus only applies while you remain behind the cover and may not apply if the enemy has a clear angle around it. + Requires bonus action and 1 AP + While being in active cover and agreed upon by the gm, you get a 10% boost to your dodge and a 5% to your reaction.

name: Good Eyes (T1)
description: Your vision and spatial awareness make you especially effective with thrown objects. You gain +5 meters of range when throwing items, including throwable weapons such as knives, grenades, or other improvised objects.

name: Alert (T1)
description: You maintain a constant level of awareness and preparedness, rarely allowing yourself to be caught completely off guard. When a situation would normally result in you being surprised, ambushed, or unprepared for combat. While this does not make you impossible to surprise, it makes it significantly harder for enemies to catch you completely unaware. What the GM does - They make a roll for the anyone with this skill, and only this skill It’s up to the GM to decide what's success and failure.

name: Eagle Eye (T2)
chain: Good Eyes + Alert -> Eagle Eye
description: Your marksmanship has evolved beyond simple perception. You gain increased effective range with firearms, allowing you to accurately engage targets at distances beyond what most shooters can manage. This ability is considered the advanced form of Good Eyes and reflects exceptional targeting skill and long distance precision. + Gaining a permanent boost to all guns by a 5 meter range and a permanent 10% to your reaction at all times.

name: Gun-Fu (T2)
description: You have trained to use firearms as both ranged and close combat weapons. Your guns can be used as blunt melee weapons when striking an opponent at close range. (Unless you have a melee based attachment on the weapon there isn’t a bonus on it.) Additionally, if you are grappled or restrained, you may attempt to fire your gun point blank as part of your action/reaction to break free. Your firearm attack can be used as the output for the escape attempt, representing a desperate but effective counterattack.

name: Lesser Reload (T2)
chain: Lesser Reload -> Veteran Reload
description: You have trained enough with firearms that reloading becomes second nature. You may reload a firearm using a bonus action instead of a normal action. This allows you to maintain pressure in combat while keeping your weapon ready. This ability represents practiced familiarity with your weapon and efficient ammunition handling developed through repeated use.

name: Lesser Reload (T2)
chain: Lesser Reload -> Veteran Reload
description: You have trained enough with firearms that reloading becomes second nature. You may reload a firearm using a bonus action instead of a normal action. This allows you to maintain pressure in combat while keeping your weapon ready. This ability represents practiced familiarity with your weapon and efficient ammunition handling developed through repeated use.

name: Artillery Gunner (T2)
chain: Artillery Gunner -> Master Artillerist
description: You have learned the basic operation of heavy weaponry such as cannons, mounted guns, and other large scale firearms. This training includes understanding loading procedures, aiming methods, and the coordination often required to operate these powerful machines effectively. While artillery may be intimidating to the inexperienced, you possess the knowledge needed to use it without hesitation. This skill allows you to operate artillery equipment more effectively and reduces the chance of errors while handling heavy weapons. Your understanding of their mechanics improves your ability to aim and fire these powerful devices during combat. If in a scene where artillery is said to be there you have access to use it as an action.

name: Gunmaster’s Flow (T3)
chain: During a combat encounter or scene, every enemy you defeat grants you a small stacking bonus to firearm related attacks. This represents your growing confidence, improved timing, and increasing control over the battlefield.
description: The effect only lasts for the duration of the encounter and resets once the scene ends.This ability reflects a combatant who thrives in battle, becoming more dangerous the longer the fight continues. + For every kill that is made by them (assist do not count) the user gains a 2% stacking output and output speed bonus for every kill.

name: Veteran Reload (T3)
chain: Lesser Reload -> Veteran Reload
description: Your mastery of firearm handling allows you to reload with near effortless speed. You may reload a firearm using both of your free actions, allowing you to chamber fresh ammunition almost instantly without interrupting your combat flow. Additionally, if a weapon normally requires careful or complex reloading, your experience allows you to perform it with the same speed and confidence as a standard firearm.

name: Master Artillerist (T3)
chain: Artillery Gunner -> Master Artillerist
description: You have achieved exceptional mastery over heavy weaponry and large scale firearms. Few individuals possess the knowledge required to fully control artillery in the chaos of battle, but you understand both its mechanical operation and its devastating strategic potential. With careful calculation and experience, you can unleash immense destructive force with remarkable precision. This mastery greatly improves your ability to aim and control artillery fire, ensuring that each shot has maximum effectiveness. You can better predict blast impact, manage trajectory, and coordinate bombardments in ways that can dramatically shift the outcome of large engagements. You can now have access to mobile artillery firearms.

name: Obedience
description: This skill allows advanced commands to be given to mounts and pets in non-combat contexts. Advanced commands allow animals to perform tricks or commands that they otherwise would not naturally do, within reason for the animal being commanded. ● Normal animals still have "animal" intelligence. ● Only Loyal Companion animals have sentience, and can follow more 'higher order' multi-step commands. EXAMPLE ● Sending a monkey into another space to unlock a door or window; teaching a rat to steal from someone's pocket; having a dog stand guard outside a door and alert when someone approaches. ● "Obedience" is not required for simple commands and tricks that wouldn't require advanced training (eg., sit, stay, speak, fetch, etc). Tier 1 OC Skills

name: Proficient Command
chain: Proficient Command -> Advanced Command
description: Through coordination and clear battlefield signals, the user has developed a strong bond and understanding with their mount or companion. This allows the user to direct their mount or pet more efficiently in combat. This represents the growing synergy between master and companion, allowing them to act in tandem rather than waiting for separate openings. + Bonus Action How It Works + By using their bonus action, the user may command their mount or pet to perform one action during the turn. + That can be used for an action or reaction.

name: Mauling
chain: N/A
description: A specialized grappling technique unique to Beastmasters, where the user commands their pet or mount to aggressively pin down a target. When initiating a maul, the companion attempts a grapple combined with an attack. If the attack successfully lands, it deals its normal damage and the target becomes grappled/held down by the companion. While a target is being mauled, they may use their action to respond in one of two ways: ● Defend Against the Assault, preventing the companion from dealing further damage that turn, or ● Attempt to Break Free, rolling their output against the companion’s output. If the target’s output exceeds the companion’s, they successfully escape the maul but suffer T1 damage during the struggle. While maintaining a maul, the pet or mount is fully committed to the grapple and cannot perform any other actions or reactions. The companion may only deal damage through its action while continuing the maul, representing its focus on keeping the target restrained and under constant pressure. + Bonus Action, 1 AP Mount’s Grapple Power = (STR x 1.2) + (STA + 0.6) + Masters (STRx.5) !IMPORTANT! - While maintaining a grapple with a conscious combatant, your Reaction Score and Attack Speed take a 5% penalty. This does not impact MAGIC restraints. Tier 2 OC Skills

name: Advanced Command
chain: Proficient Command -> Advanced Command
description: The bond between the user and their mount or companion has reached a level of near instinctive understanding. The mount or pet is capable of acting with far greater independence while still responding to the master’s direction. How It Works + The companion now gains access to its own action and reaction, separate from its master’s turn economy. + In addition, the companion may still utilize the master’s bonus action slot when commanded, allowing for greater tactical flexibility and coordinated maneuvers in combat.

name: Shared Experience
chain: Shared Experience -> Shared Experience II
description: Through time spent traveling, fighting, and growing alongside their master, the companion begins to develop and learn from those experiences. As long as the companion is present with the user during scenes where experience is earned, they will begin accumulating growth of their own. ● The companion gains 10% of the experience the user earns whenever XP is awarded. This experience is tracked separately and cannot be transferred to the OC under any circumstances. The experience gained is solely for the companion’s own progression and development.

name: Remote Sense
chain: Remote Sense -> Spectral Bond
description: The bond between the user and their companion grows strong enough that distance alone cannot sever their awareness of one another. No matter how far apart they may be, both the user and their companion can sense the other’s presence across long distances. While this connection does not allow for spoken communication or direct conversation, it allows both parties to feel one another’s emotions, intentions, and distress. Feelings such as pain, fear, urgency, or comfort can be sensed through the bond, allowing the pair to remain connected even when physically separated. ● You and your companion are always able to locate one another. Your companion can act fully independently when separated from you, and can still take advantage of shared bonuses. You and your companion can 'sense' what the other is doing, feeling, or experiencing. ○ This does not mean your pet gets "meta", but they will have a rudimentary understanding of your will. Tier 3 OC Skills

name: Shared Experience II
prereq_raw: Pet or Mount in scene, Shared Experience
chain: Shared Experience -> Shared Experience II
description: Through time spent traveling, fighting, and growing alongside their master, the companion begins to develop and learn from those experiences. As long as the companion is present with the user during scenes where experience is earned, they will begin accumulating growth of their own. The companion gains 20% of the experience the user earns whenever XP is awarded. This experience is tracked separately and cannot be transferred to the OC under any circumstances. The experience gained is solely for the companion’s own progression and development.

name: Spectral Bond
chain: Remote Sense -> Spectral Bond
description: The connection between the user and their companion evolves beyond simple emotional awareness, allowing the companion to briefly manifest through the bond itself. By channeling this link, the companion can shift into a spectral, ghost-like form, becoming incorporeal for a short duration. In this state, the companion can phase through solid objects such as walls, barriers, or terrain, allowing it to scout, reposition, or escape otherwise impossible situations. + 3 AP, Bonus Action ● While in this form, the companion cannot physically interact with the environment or perform physical attacks. At any point during this state, the companion may instantly return to their master, teleporting back to them as long as they are within a 10 meter range.. This evolution of the bond makes separation far less dangerous, ensuring the companion can always find its way back to its partner. ○ However in this form any boost gained or related to Loyal Companion trait & its skills are voided. For both the master and the pet. Source Beast Skills The Source Beast Skills are skills for your Loyal Companion ONLY. THIS SECTION IS BEING REVIEWED. Until further notice, Beast Skills are NOT purchasable; they will be released as soon as we complete balancing for OC-based skills. Understanding Beast Skills There are 3 types of Beasts (which includes both Source Beasts from Loyal Companion and non-Source beasts). ● Combat Pets ● Mount Pets ● Support Pets The type of pet/companion you have will determine what you can do with them and what sort of skills they can have. Please review the Pet Guide for more information. Non-Source Beasts Non-source beasts are easily known to be different from their Source Beast counterparts based on appearance and the fact that Source Beast don;t exist in the wild. They as well don’t have the ability/capacity to go into the other skill trees. Example for Non-Source Beast vs Source Beast: - If you gained a wolf it could have the options between Combat or Support you must make that choice and only be stuck into the combat path line. - If you had a Source Beast that resembled a wolf it could be any of the 3 paths and still have access to the other trees to the extent based on path chosen. Source Beasts (and Loyal Companions) The skills your Loyal Companion can obtain are determined by the type of beast they are. Each category has a different maximum “cap” on skills. ●Combat Pets ○ Combat: Tier 3 (Primary Strength) ○ Mount: Tier 1 (Tertiary Role) ○ Support: Tier 2 (Secondary Role) ●Mount Pets ○ Combat: Tier 1 (Tertiary Role) ○ Mount: Tier 3 (Primary Strength) ○ Support: Tier 2 (Secondary Role) ●Support Pets ○ Combat: Tier 2 (Secondary Role) ○ Mount: Tier 1 (Tertiary Role) ○ Support: Tier 3 (Primary Strength) Beast Skills - Combat Skills Tier 1

name: Battle Bond, Bonus Action
chain: Battle Bond -> Bonded Soul
description: The connection between you and your companion strengthens in the heat of battle. When both you and your companion focus the same target, your companion gains a +5% output bonus to their attacks.

name: Magical Attack I, Passive
chain: Magical Attack I -> Magical Attack II -> Magical Attack III
description: Your companion begins to awaken latent magical potential, allowing it to perform basic offensive spells. It gains access to Tier 1 Offensive Magic, restricted to the weak power band, such as minor elemental bursts, energy projectiles, or simple magical strikes.

name: Hound Strike, Bonus Action
description: Your companion executes a trained, aggressive strike designed to capitalize on openings. This attack gains a +15% output bonus but the beast gets a tier 1 injury.

name: Increased Physicality I {Combat}, Passive
description: Your companion’s combat capabilities are slightly enhanced through conditioning or natural growth. Choose one attribute: Strength, Dexterity, or Magical Affinity. That stat gains a permanent +5% increase. Tier 2

name: Bonded Soul, Bonus Action
chain: Battle Bond -> Bonded Soul
description: The connection between you and your companion deepens, allowing for sharper coordination in battle. When attacking the same target as your master, your companion gains a +10% output bonus, reflecting heightened synergy and shared intent.

name: Magical Attack II, Passive
chain: Magical Attack I -> Magical Attack II -> Magical Attack III
description: Your companion’s offensive magic grows in strength and control. Its Offensive Magic upgrades to the standard power band, allowing for more reliable and impactful spellcasting while maintaining Tier 1 limitations.

name: Hound Strike, Bonus Action
description: Your companion executes a trained, aggressive strike designed to capitalize on openings. This attack gains a +15% output bonus but the beast gets a tier 1 injury.

name: Increased Physicality I {Combat}, Passive

That stat gains a permanent +5% increase. Tier 2 1. Magic Combat, Passive a. Your companion expands its magical repertoire, gaining access to either: i. Weakening Magic (debuffs, impairments), or ii. Summoning Magic (minor conjurations or entities) b. All abilities are limited to the standard power band, offering utility and battlefield control. 2. Increased Physicality II (Combat), Passive a. Continued training enhances your companion’s natural combat strengths. The previously chosen attribute (Strength, Dexterity, or Magical Affinity) is further improved, increasing its bonus to +7% total. b. Requires: Increased Physicality I (Combat) 3. Helping Hand I, Passive a. Your companion learns to fight alongside others beyond just its master. When attacking the same target as an approved ally, it gains a +7% output bonus. The master determines which allies qualify, reinforcing tactical teamwork. 4. Source Exchange, Bonus Action a. Your companion can convert its own physical endurance into energy for its master. By willingly taking damage, it generates AP for the master, enabling more advanced abilities. The amount gained depends on the severity of the injury: i. Tier 1 Injury: +1 AP ii. Tier 2 Injury: +2 AP iii. Tier 3 Injury: +3 AP Tier 3 1. Ravaged Strike - Your companion executes a mastered strike designed to incapacitate the target. This attack gains a +25% output bonus and if it lands it gains a bleeding effect, with no injury gained by the user anymore. a. |Requires Hound Strike| 2. Magical Attack III - Offense magic upgrades to its max capacity and now can create AOE attacks, following the power band however stays at standard. |Requires Magical Attack II| 3. The master determines which allies qualify, reinforcing tactical teamwork. b. Requires |Helping Hand I| 4. Increased Physicality II (Combat), Passive a. Requires: Increased Physicality II (Combat) Beast Skills - Mount Skills Tier 1 1. Increased Inventory, Passive a. Your companion is trained or equipped to carry additional supplies. It gains +4 inventory capacity (or slots), allowing for more gear, materials, or items to be transported without hindering performance. 2. Magical Reinforcement I, Passive a. Your companion gains access to basic reinforcement magic, enhancing physical performance through magical means. b. It can use Tier 1 Reinforcement Magic within the weak power band. 3. One Unit, Passive a. Rider and mount move as a single, coordinated entity. While mounted, your defensive responsiveness improves by combining your instincts: b. Use your Dodge and Reaction + 50% of your companion’s Dodge and Reaction. 4. Increased Physicality I (Mount), Passive a. Your companion’s endurance is improved for sustained activity. It gains a +7% increase to Stamina, allowing it to travel longer distances, endure extended combat scenarios, and better resist fatigue-related penalties. Tier 2 1. Scouting, Bonus Action a. Your companion becomes capable of advanced traversal while carrying you. It can fly or swim with you mounted while maintaining its normal movement speed. However, combat actions cannot be performed while using this ability, and it must logically align with the creature’s form. 2. Magical Reinforcement II, Passive a. Your companion’s reinforcement magic improves, upgrading to the standard power band. This allows for stronger enhancements to speed, durability, or physical output. b. Requires: Magical Reinforcement I 3. Magical Change, Passive a. Your companion gains access to transformative magic, choosing one: i. Area Transmutation, or ii. Transformation (limited to mobility-based changes such as flight, gills, or movement adaptations) b. All abilities are restricted to the standard power band. 4. Increased Physicality II (Mount), Bonus Action a. Your companion’s endurance continues to grow. Its Stamina bonus increases to +10% total, improving travel longevity and resistance to fatigue. b. Requires: Increased Physicality I (Mount) 5. Escape Artist, Bonus Action a. When retreat becomes necessary, you and your mount act with unmatched urgency. By committing your entire action for the round, you and one additional rider can disengage and escape. Distance and travel time still apply, and once dismissed, the mount requires time before it can return. 6. Rider & Steed (Source Beast Exclusive), Bonus Action a. The bond between you and your companion blurs the line between separate beings. You may transfer injuries between yourself, your companion, or another target, redistributing damage strategically. This reflects a powerful and unnatural unity. Tier 3 1. True Rider, Bonus Action a. Requires: Magical Reinforcement II 4. Unified, Passive a. Rider and mount move as a single, coordinated entity. While mounted, your defensive responsiveness improves by combining your instincts. b. Use your Dodge and Reaction + 50% of your companion’s Dodge and Reactio

chain: Water Knowledge → Water Attunement → Water Mastery → Water Sorcery
description: Skill checks on Water Sourcery and natural water-based skills gain +2; allows simple water-based magics.

chain: Water Knowledge → Water Attunement → Water Mastery → Water Sorcery

chain: Water Knowledge → Water Attunement → Water Mastery → Water Sorcery

description: Wind spells may add flight as an extra effect for free to any single-target spell that makes sense; Transmutation and Alteration spells cost 10% less.
